close_reason: Accepted. run_on_new_thread is pub because the umbrella crate needs it. Inlining would duplicate the implementation. The one-thread-per-call strategy is documented and correct for the use case.
description: run_on_new_thread in secretx-core/src/lib.rs is pub under the blocking feature flag. By making it pub, the crate commits to the specific implementation strategy (one OS thread per call, current_thread Tokio runtime) as a stable public API. Callers may take a dependency on this function being available and having these exact semantics. What goes wrong: if the implementation needs to change (e.g. to use a blocking thread pool instead of always spawning a new thread), that is now a breaking change to the public API even if the semantics are equivalent. The function is an internal implementation detail of get_blocking and should not be separately pub unless there is a documented reason callers need it directly. Fix: make run_on_new_thread pub(crate) and expose only get_blocking as the public API, or add explicit documentation stating this is an intentionally public escape hatch with stable semantics.
title: run_on_new_thread pub exposes internal blocking implementation strategy
